373	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian
374	
375	/-! ## The universal quotient sheaf and the tautological quotient -/
376	
377	/-- The **universal quotient sheaf** `U` on `Gr(d,r)` (`def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf`):
378	the rank-`d` locally free sheaf obtained by gluing the free rank-`d` chart sheaves
379	`O_{U^I}^d` along the bundle transition cocycle `g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)⁻¹`.
380	
381	NOTE: `Scheme.Modules.glue` has landed (axiom-clean, via the descent equalizer of
382	pushforwards). The remaining obligation is the **GL_d bundle transition cocycle**, which
383	is net-new infrastructure not reachable from `glue` alone:
384	`universalQuotient d r := Scheme.Modules.glue (theGlueData d r)`
385	`  (fun I => SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I.1).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) g hC1 hC2`
386	where `g I J : (pullback (chartIncl I J))^* O^d ≅ (pullback (t I J ≫ chartIncl J I))^* O^d` is
387	the matrix automorphism of free sheaves induced by `universalMinorInv I J = (X^I_J)⁻¹`
388	(built like `chartQuotientMap`: `scalarEnd` of the matrix entries over the overlap ring,
389	assembled over the rank-`d` biproduct, conjugated by `pullbackFreeIso`), and `hC1`/`hC2` are
390	the module-level self-identity and triple-overlap multiplicativity — the `GL_d`-valued
391	cocycle, transported via `pullbackBaseChangeTransport`/`glueData_bridge_*`. -/
392	noncomputable def universalQuotient (d r : ℕ) : (scheme d r).Modules :=
393	  sorry
394	
395	/-- The **tautological quotient** `u : O^r ↠ U` (`def:tautological_quotient`): the global
396	surjection assembled from the chart quotients `u^I` (`chartQuotientMap`), compatible with
397	the bundle gluing of `universalQuotient`.
398	
399	NOTE: rides on `universalQuotient` (hence on the bundle cocycle, the only remaining gap;
400	`Scheme.Modules.glue` itself has landed). Assembled from the chart quotients `u^I`
401	(`chartQuotientMap`) glued along the same cocycle via `Scheme.Modules.glueHom`. -/
402	noncomputable def tautologicalQuotient (d r : ℕ) :
403	    SheafOfModules.free (R := (scheme d r).ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶ universalQuotient d r :=
404	  sorry

406	/-! ## The functor of points and the universal property
407	
408	The Grassmannian functor sends a scheme `T` to the set of *equivalence classes* of
409	rank-`d` quotients `q : O_T^r ↠ F` with `F` locally free of rank `d`. We encode a single
410	such quotient as the structure `RankQuotient r d T`, the equivalence as `RankQuotient.Rel`
411	(an isomorphism of the targets commuting with the quotient maps), and the functor's value
412	at `T` as the quotient `Quotient (rqSetoid r d T)`. The pullback action `rqPullback`
413	together with `pullbackFreeIso`/`pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` (and the fact that the
414	pullback functor preserves epimorphisms, being a left adjoint) realise functoriality.

50	polynomial ring `MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ` in the `d(r-d)`
51	free entries of the standard matrix representative with `I`-minor equal to the
52	identity. This chart is non-canonically isomorphic to `𝔸^{d(r-d)}_ℤ`.
53	
54	The Grassmannian scheme is obtained by gluing the `Nat.choose r d` affine charts
55	`U^I` along the Plücker cocycle transition maps. -/
56	noncomputable def affineChart (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) : Scheme :=
57	  AlgebraicGeometry.Spec (CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ))
58	
59	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Grassmannian transition maps
60	
61	The transition map `θ_{I,J}` of `def:gr_transition` is a Cramer-inverse computation
62	over the localised chart ring `R^I_J := ℤ[X^I, 1/P^I_J]`, followed by the universal
63	property of the away-localisation. We build it bottom-up over the chart ring
64	`R^I := MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ`, all elementary matrix algebra
65	over `ℤ`. The `d`-subset combinatorics are handled by the order isomorphism
66	`Finset.orderIsoOfFin : I.card = d → (Fin d ≃o ↥I)`.
67	
68	Blueprint references: `def:gr_universal_matrix`, `def:gr_minor_det`,
69	`def:gr_universal_minor`, `lem:gr_minorDet_unit`, `def:gr_universalMinorInv`,
70	`lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities`, `def:gr_image_matrix`, `def:gr_transition_pre`
71	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
72	
73	/-- The **universal matrix** `X^I` (`def:gr_universal_matrix`): the `d × r` matrix
74	over the chart ring `R^I = MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q // q ∉ I}) ℤ` whose `I`-minor is the
75	`d × d` identity (read through the order iso `Fin d ≃o ↥I`) and whose remaining
76	`d(r-d)` entries are the free indeterminates `x^I_{p,q}` (`q ∉ I`). Project-local:
77	the universal point of the affine chart `affineChart d r I`. -/
78	noncomputable def universalMatrix (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
79	    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) :=
80	  fun p q =>
81	    if h : q ∈ I then (if (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p : Fin r) = q then 1 else 0)
82	    else MvPolynomial.X (p, ⟨q, h⟩)
83	
84	/-- The **minor determinant** `P^I_J = det(X^I_J)` (`def:gr_minor_det`): the determinant
85	of the `d × d` submatrix of `universalMatrix d r I` whose columns are those indexed by
86	`J`, reindexed to `Fin d` via the order iso `Fin d ≃o ↥J`. Project-local: defines the
87	principal open `U^I_J = Spec R^I[1/P^I_J]`. -/
88	noncomputable def minorDet (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
89	    (hJ : J.card = d) : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ :=
90	  ((universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id
91	    (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).det
92	
93	/-- The **localised `J`-minor** `X^I_J` over `R^I_J` (`def:gr_universal_minor`): the
94	`J`-minor of `universalMatrix d r I` with entries pushed forward along the structure map
95	`R^I → R^I_J = Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J)`. Project-local. -/
96	noncomputable def universalMinor (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
97	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
98	    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
99	  ((universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id
100	    (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).map (algebraMap _ _)
101	
102	/-- The localised minor determinant is a unit (`lem:gr_minorDet_unit`): `det(X^I_J)` is the
103	image of `P^I_J` under the away-localisation structure map, hence a unit. Project-local. -/
104	theorem isUnit_det_universalMinor (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
105	    (hJ : J.card = d) : IsUnit (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det := by
106	  have h : (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det
107	      = (algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ))) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
108	    (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm
109	  rw [h]
110	  exact IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _
111	
112	/-- The **Cramer inverse** `(X^I_J)⁻¹` (`def:gr_universalMinorInv`): the Mathlib
113	nonsingular inverse of the localised `J`-minor. Its entries lie in `R^I_J` by Cramer's
114	rule. Project-local. -/
115	noncomputable def universalMinorInv (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
116	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
117	    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
118	  (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)⁻¹
119	
120	/-- The Cramer inverse is a two-sided inverse (`lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities`):
121	since `det(X^I_J)` is a unit, `(X^I_J)⁻¹` is a genuine left and right inverse.
122	Project-local. -/
123	theorem universalMinorInv_mul_cancel (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
124	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
125	    universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ * universalMinor d r I J hI hJ = 1 ∧
126	    universalMinor d r I J hI hJ * universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ = 1 :=
127	  ⟨Matrix.nonsing_inv_mul _ (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I J hI hJ),
128	   Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv _ (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)⟩
129	
130	/-- The **image matrix** `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` (`def:gr_image_matrix`): the product of the
131	Cramer inverse with the universal matrix base-changed to `R^I_J`. Its entries are the
132	prospective images of the indeterminates `x^J_{p,q}` under `θ_{I,J}`. Project-local. -/
133	noncomputable def imageMatrix (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
134	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
135	    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
136	  universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ * (universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)
137	
138	/-- The **pre-localisation hom** `θ̃_{I,J} : R^J → R^I_J` (`def:gr_transition_pre`): the
139	`ℤ`-algebra map out of the chart ring of `J` sending each free indeterminate `x^J_{p,q}`
140	to the `(p,q)`-entry of the image matrix `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I`. Project-local. -/
141	noncomputable def transitionPreMap (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
142	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
143	    MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ →ₐ[ℤ]
144	      Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
145	  MvPolynomial.aeval (fun e => imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ e.1 e.2.1)
146	
147	/-- The `I`-minor of the universal matrix `X^I` is the `d × d` identity (`P^I_I = 1`
148	underlies `lem:gr_transition_self`): the `I`-columns of `X^I` read through the order iso
149	`Fin d ≃o ↥I` form the identity block. Project-local. -/
150	theorem universalMatrix_submatrix_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
151	    (universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id
152	      (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) = 1 := by
153	  ext p p'
154	  simp only [Matrix.submatrix_apply, id_eq, universalMatrix]
155	  rw [dif_pos (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p').2, Matrix.one_apply]
156	  have hiff : ((I.orderIsoOfFin hI p : Fin r) = (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p')) ↔ (p = p') := by
157	    rw [Subtype.coe_inj, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq]
158	  by_cases h : p = p'
159	  · rw [if_pos (hiff.mpr h), if_pos h]
160	  · rw [if_neg (hiff.not.mpr h), if_neg h]
161	
162	/-- Submatrix on the columns of a right matrix factor commutes with the product
163	(rows reindexed by `id`). Project-local helper: avoids a matrix-multiplication
164	instance-keying issue that blocks the generic `rw` of Mathlib's submatrix-mul lemmas. -/
165	private lemma mul_submatrix_col {d r : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
166	    (A : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R) (B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) R) (g : Fin d → Fin r) :
167	    (A * B).submatrix id g = A * B.submatrix id g := by
168	  ext i j; simp [Matrix.mul_apply, Matrix.submatrix_apply]
169	
170	/-- The `J`-minor of the image matrix `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` is the identity: `M_J =
171	(X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I_J = 1` (`def:gr_image_matrix`, used in the cocycle/transition arguments).
172	Project-local. -/
173	theorem imageMatrix_submatrix_self (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
174	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
175	    (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
176	      (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r)) = 1 := by
177	  have h1 : (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
178	        (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))
179	      = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ *
180	        (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)).submatrix id
181	          (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))) := mul_submatrix_col _ _ _
182	  rw [h1, Matrix.submatrix_map]
183	  exact (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1
184

162	/-! ## Gluing a sheaf of modules along a scheme glue datum
163	
164	`Scheme.Modules.glue` descends a sheaf of modules from per-chart data plus a transition
165	cocycle over a `Scheme.GlueData`. Mathlib carries no turn-key module descent over a
166	scheme glue datum (confirmed), so this is an Archon-original construction. The
167	construction path (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue`): restrict to a chart through the
168	open-immersion pullback equivalence (`Scheme.Modules.overRestrictPullbackIso`) and glue
169	the local sections by locality of sections (`existsUnique_gluing'`).
170	
171	NOTE (scaffold): the body and the module-cocycle hypotheses on `g` are still to be filled;
172	the transition data `g` (per-overlap pullback isos) is recorded in the signature, the
173	multiplicative cocycle conditions remain to be added before the construction is closed. -/
174	
175	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
176	
177	/-! ### Base-change transport of a transition isomorphism to a triple overlap
178	
179	To state the triple-overlap multiplicativity (C2) of a module descent datum we must
180	transport each transition isomorphism `g_ij`, living on the overlap `V_ij`, to the common
181	triple overlap `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik`. The transport pulls `g_ij` back along a
182	projection `p : V_ijk ⟶ V_ij` and reassociates the iterated pullbacks via the pseudofunctor
183	comparison `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`. The three scheme-level `glueData_bridge_*`
184	identities below (consequences of `t_fac`, `pullback.condition` and `cocycle`) line up the
185	endpoints of the three transports so that the cocycle equation is well typed. -/
186	
187	/-- **Base-change transport of a transition isomorphism along a morphism**
188	(`lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport`). Given a transition isomorphism
189	`g : a^*Mᵢ ≅ b^*Mⱼ` over `V` and a morphism `p : W ⟶ V`, transport it to `W` as an
190	isomorphism `(p ≫ a)^*Mᵢ ≅ (p ≫ b)^*Mⱼ`, by pulling `g` back along `p` and reassociating
191	the iterated pullbacks through `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`.
192	
193	Project-local: this is the pullback-pseudofunctor packaging that lets the three transition
194	isomorphisms attached to a triple of charts be compared on a single triple overlap; Mathlib
195	has no descent-of-modules-over-a-scheme-glue-datum API. -/
196	noncomputable def pullbackBaseChangeTransport {W V : Scheme.{u}} (p : W ⟶ V)
197	    {Yi Yj : Scheme.{u}} (a : V ⟶ Yi) (b : V ⟶ Yj)
198	    {Mi : Yi.Modules} {Mj : Yj.Modules}
199	    (g : (Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj Mi ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj Mj) :
200	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (p ≫ a)).obj Mi ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback (p ≫ b)).obj Mj :=
201	  (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p a).symm.app Mi ≪≫
202	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).mapIso g ≪≫
203	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p b).app Mj
204	
205	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (source): on `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik` the two projections to
206	`V_ij` and `V_ik` followed by the overlap immersions `f_ij`, `f_ik` agree as morphisms to
207	`U_i`. This is the pullback condition; it identifies the sources of the `ij`- and
208	`ik`-transports. Project-local helper for the module cocycle (C2). -/
209	theorem glueData_bridge_src (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
210	    pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.f i j
211	      = pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.f i k := pullback.condition
212	
213	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (middle): the `ij`-transition's target leg
214	`p^{ij} ≫ (t_ij ≫ f_ji)` to `U_j` coincides with the `jk`-transition's source leg
215	`(t'_ijk ≫ p^{jk}) ≫ f_jk`. Follows from `t_fac` and the pullback condition; it identifies
216	the target of the `ij`-transport with the source of the `jk`-transport. Project-local helper
217	for the module cocycle (C2). -/
218	theorem glueData_bridge_mid (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
219	    pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)
220	      = (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i)) ≫ D.f j k := by
221	  rw [Category.assoc, pullback.condition, ← Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc, D.t_fac i j k,
222	    Category.assoc]
223	
224	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (target): the `jk`-transition's target leg
225	`(t'_ijk ≫ p^{jk}) ≫ (t_jk ≫ f_kj)` to `U_k` coincides with the `ik`-transition's target
226	leg `p^{ik} ≫ (t_ik ≫ f_ki)`. This is the heart of the cocycle, derived from `t_fac`, the
227	pullback condition, `t_inv` and `cocycle`; it identifies the target of the composite
228	`jk`-after-`ij` transport with the target of the `ik`-transport. Project-local helper for
229	the module cocycle (C2). -/
230	theorem glueData_bridge_tgt (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
231	    (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i)) ≫ (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j)
232	      = pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) := by
233	  have key : pullback.fst (D.f k i) (D.f k j) ≫ D.f k i
234	      = D.t' k i j ≫ pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.t i k ≫ D.f k i := by
235	    rw [D.t_fac_assoc k i j, ← Category.assoc (D.t k i) (D.t i k), D.t_inv, Category.id_comp]
236	  rw [Category.assoc, ← D.t_fac_assoc j k i,
237	    ← @pullback.condition _ _ _ _ _ (D.f k i) (D.f k j) _, key, D.cocycle_assoc i j k]
238	
239	/-- **Gluing a sheaf of modules along an open cover given by a scheme glue datum**
240	(`def:scheme_modules_glue`). From a glue datum `D`, per-chart sheaves of modules `M i`,
241	and transition isomorphisms `g i j` comparing the two charts' sheaves over the overlap
242	`V (i,j)` (after pullback), produces a glued sheaf of `O_{D.glued}`-modules.
243	
244	Project-local: Mathlib has no module descent over a scheme glue datum. -/
245	noncomputable def glue (D : Scheme.GlueData)
246	    (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)
247	    (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
248	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j))
249	    -- (C1) self-identity: over the diagonal overlap `V (i,i)` (where `t i i = 𝟙`) the
250	    -- transition isomorphism is the identity, i.e. the canonical isomorphism induced by
251	    -- `f i i = t i i ≫ f i i` (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue` (C1)).
252	    (_hC1 : ∀ i, g i i = eqToIso (congrArg (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (M i))
253	        (show D.f i i = D.t i i ≫ D.f i i by rw [D.t_id i, Category.id_comp])))
254	    -- (C2) triple-overlap multiplicativity: over each triple overlap
255	    -- `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik` the base-change transports
256	    -- (`pullbackBaseChangeTransport`) of the three transition isomorphisms `g_ij`, `g_jk`,
257	    -- `g_ik` satisfy `ĝ_jk ∘ ĝ_ij = ĝ_ik`. The three `glueData_bridge_*` identities, applied
258	    -- through `pullbackCongr`, line up the endpoints so the equation is well typed
259	    -- (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue` (C2), `lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport`).
260	    (_hC2 : ∀ i j k,
261	        pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
262	            (D.f i j) (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (g i j) ≪≫
263	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_mid D i j k)).app (M j) ≪≫
264	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i))
265	            (D.f j k) (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j) (g j k) ≪≫
266	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_tgt D i j k)).app (M k)
267	        = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_src D i j k)).app (M i) ≪≫
268	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
269	            (D.f i k) (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) (g i k)) :
270	    D.glued.Modules :=
271	  -- **Effective descent as an equalizer of pushforwards.** The glued sheaf is the
272	  -- equalizer of the two canonical maps `∏ᵢ (ιᵢ)_* Mᵢ ⇉ ∏_{ij} (j_ij)_* (f_ij^* Mᵢ)`
273	  -- (`j_ij = f_ij ≫ ιᵢ : V_ij ↪ X`): the first map restricts the `i`-th chart section to
274	  -- `V_ij`, the second restricts the `j`-th and transports it across the transition `g_ij`,
275	  -- using the glue condition `(t_ij ≫ f_ji) ≫ ιⱼ = f_ij ≫ ιᵢ`. The cocycle hypotheses
276	  -- `_hC1`/`_hC2` are not needed to *construct* the object (they pin down the chart
277	  -- restriction isomorphisms `glueRestrictionIso`, built downstream). Pushforward preserves
278	  -- the sheaf condition and limits, so this equalizer of sheaves of modules is again a sheaf.
279	  let Qfun : D.J × D.J → D.glued.Modules := fun p =>
280	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1)).obj
281	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))
282	  let P : D.glued.Modules := ∏ᶜ fun i => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι i)).obj (M i)
283	  -- first leg: restrict the `p.1`-chart section to the overlap `V (p.1, p.2)`
284	  let aComp : ∀ p : D.J × D.J,
285	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).obj (M p.1) ⟶ Qfun p := fun p =>
286	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).map
287	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f p.1 p.2)).unit.app (M p.1)) ≫
288	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.f p.1 p.2) (D.ι p.1)).hom.app
289	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))
290	  -- second leg: restrict the `p.2`-chart section, transport it across `g`, and reindex
291	  -- the immersion via the glue condition
292	  let bComp : ∀ p : D.J × D.J,
293	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).obj (M p.2) ⟶ Qfun p := fun p =>
294	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).map
295	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
296	          (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).unit.app (M p.2)) ≫
297	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) (D.ι p.2)).hom.app
298	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).obj (M p.2)) ≫
299	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
300	        ((D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2)).map (g p.1 p.2).inv ≫
301	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
302	        (show (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2 = D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1 by
303	          rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition p.1 p.2)).hom.app
304	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))
305	  let a : P ⟶ ∏ᶜ Qfun := Pi.lift fun p => Pi.π _ p.1 ≫ aComp p
306	  let b : P ⟶ ∏ᶜ Qfun := Pi.lift fun p => Pi.π _ p.2 ≫ bComp p
307	  equalizer a b
308	
309	/-! ### Pullback of free sheaves along an arbitrary scheme morphism
310	
311	The functor of points (`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor`) acts on morphisms by
312	pullback, and for that one needs `f^* (O^n) ≅ O^n` for an *arbitrary* scheme morphism
313	`f`. Mathlib's `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` supplies this only when the underlying
314	site functor `Opens.map f.base` is `Final`; the previous chapters discharged that `Final`
315	hypothesis only for open immersions and isomorphisms. The first lemma below removes the
316	restriction entirely: `Opens.map f.base` is `Final` for *every* scheme morphism, because

1133	cocycle into `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData`, indexed by the size-`d` subsets
1134	`{I : Finset (Fin r) // I.card = d}`; its `.glued` is the Grassmannian scheme `Gr(d,r)`
1135	over `ℤ`. Blueprint reference: `def:gr_glued_scheme`. -/
1136	
1137	/-- The **Grassmannian glue data** (`def:gr_glued_scheme`): the `Scheme.GlueData` whose
1138	charts are `affineChart`, overlaps `chartOverlap`, inclusions `chartIncl`, transitions
1139	`chartTransition`, with cocycle `chartTransition'_cocycle`. Indexed by the size-`d` subsets
1140	of `Fin r`. Project-local. -/
1141	noncomputable def theGlueData (d r : ℕ) : Scheme.GlueData where
1142	  J := {I : Finset (Fin r) // I.card = d}
1143	  U I := affineChart d r I.1
1144	  V p := chartOverlap d r p.1.1 p.2.1 p.1.2 p.2.2
1145	  f I J := chartIncl d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2
1146	  f_id I := chartIncl_self_isIso d r I.1 I.2
1147	  f_open I J := chartIncl_isOpenImmersion d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2
1148	  t I J := chartTransition d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2
1149	  t_id I := chartTransition_self d r I.1 I.2
1150	  t' I J K := chartTransition' d r I.1 J.1 K.1 I.2 J.2 K.2
1151	  t_fac I J K := chartTransition'_fac d r I.1 J.1 K.1 I.2 J.2 K.2
1152	  cocycle I J K := chartTransition'_cocycle d r I.1 J.1 K.1 I.2 J.2 K.2
1153	
1154	/-- The **Grassmannian scheme** `Gr(d,r)` over `ℤ` (`def:gr_glued_scheme`): the scheme glued
1155	from the `Nat.choose r d` affine charts `U^I` along the Plücker transition isomorphisms.
1156	Project-local. -/
1157	noncomputable def scheme (d r : ℕ) : Scheme :=
1158	  (theGlueData d r).glued
1159	
1160	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the restricted diagonal and separatedness
1161	
1162	Following Nitsure §1, "Separatedness": the Grassmannian is separated over `ℤ` because on
1163	each affine patch `U^I ×_ℤ U^J` of `Gr(d,r) ×_ℤ Gr(d,r)` the restricted diagonal is a
1164	closed immersion, equivalently the comorphism ring map
1165	`δ_{I,J} : ℤ[X^I] ⊗_ℤ ℤ[X^J] → R^I_J = ℤ[X^I, 1/P^I_J]`,
1166	`X^I ⊗ 1 ↦ X^I`, `1 ⊗ X^J ↦ (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` (the comorphism `θ_{I,J}`), is surjective.
1167	We build `δ_{I,J}` as the tensor-product lift of the structure map `R^I → R^I_J` and the
1168	pre-localisation transition hom `θ̃_{I,J}`, and prove it surjective: its image contains
1169	`X^I` (left factor) and `1/P^I_J = δ_{I,J}(1 ⊗ P^J_I)` (the `I`-minor determinant of
1170	`(X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I`), hence all of `R^I_J`.
1171	
1172	Blueprint reference: `lem:gr_separated`
1173	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
1174	
1175	/-- The pre-hom sends `P^J_I` to the multiplicative inverse of (the image of) `P^I_J`:
1176	`θ̃_{I,J}(P^J_I) · P^I_J = 1` in `R^I_J`. This refines `isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet`
1177	to the explicit two-sided inverse, used to realise `1/P^I_J` in the image of the diagonal
1178	ring map. Project-local. -/
1179	theorem transitionPreMap_minorDet_swap_mul (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1180	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
1181	    transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) *
1182	        algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1183	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) = 1 := by
1184	  have e1 : transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)
1185	      = (((universalMatrix d r J hJ).submatrix id
1186	          (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))).map
1187	            (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ)).det :=
1188	    RingHom.map_det (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom _
1189	  rw [e1, ← Matrix.submatrix_map, universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap, imageMatrix_submatrix_I]
1190	  have hu : (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det
1191	      = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1192	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=

975	/-- Convert a (dependently typed) matrix to a morphism of biproducts. -/
976	def biproduct.matrix (m : ∀ j k, f j ⟶ g k) : ⨁ f ⟶ ⨁ g :=
977	  biproduct.desc fun j => biproduct.lift fun k => m j k

981	    biproduct.matrix m ≫ biproduct.π g k = biproduct.desc fun j => m j k := by

987	    biproduct.ι f j ≫ biproduct.matrix m = biproduct.lift fun k => m j k := by

12	/-!
13	# Free sheaves of modules
14	
15	In this file, we construct the functor
16	`SheafOfModules.freeFunctor : Type u ⥤ SheafOfModules.{u} R` which sends
17	a type `I` to the coproduct of copies indexed by `I` of `unit R`.
18	
19	## TODO
20	
21	* In case the category `C` has a terminal object `X`, promote `freeHomEquiv`
22	  into an adjunction between `freeFunctor` and the evaluation functor at `X`.
23	  (Alternatively, assuming specific universe parameters, we could show that
24	  `freeFunctor` is a left adjoint to `SheafOfModules.sectionsFunctor`.)
25	
26	-/
27	
28	@[expose] public section
29	
30	universe u v₁ v₂ u₁ u₂
31	open CategoryTheory Limits
32	
33	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}
34	  [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{u}] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
35	  [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
36	
37	namespace SheafOfModules
38	
39	/-- The free sheaf of modules on a certain type `I`. -/
40	noncomputable def free (I : Type u) : SheafOfModules.{u} R := ∐ (fun (_ : I) ↦ unit R)
41	
42	/-- The inclusions `unit R ⟶ free I`. -/
43	noncomputable def ιFree {I : Type u} (i : I) : unit R ⟶ free I :=
44	  Sigma.ι (fun (_ : I) ↦ unit R) i
45	
46	/-- The tautological cofan with point `free I : SheafOfModules R`. -/
47	noncomputable def freeCofan (I : Type u) : Cofan (fun (_ : I) ↦ unit R) :=
48	  Cofan.mk (P := free I) ιFree
49	
50	@[simp]
51	lemma freeCofan_inj {I : Type u} (i : I) :
52	    (freeCofan (R := R) I).inj i = ιFree i := rfl
53	
54	/-- `free I` is the colimit of copies of `unit R` indexed by `I`. -/
55	noncomputable def isColimitFreeCofan (I : Type u) :
56	    IsColimit (freeCofan (R := R) I) :=
57	  coproductIsCoproduct _
58	
59	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
60	/-- The data of a morphism `free I ⟶ M` from a free sheaf of modules is
61	equivalent to the data of a family `I → M.sections` of sections of `M`. -/
62	noncomputable def freeHomEquiv (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) {I : Type u} :
63	    (free I ⟶ M) ≃ (I → M.sections) where
64	  toFun f i := M.unitHomEquiv (ιFree i ≫ f)
65	  invFun s := Cofan.IsColimit.desc (isColimitFreeCofan I) (fun i ↦ M.unitHomEquiv.symm (s i))
66	  left_inv s := Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext (isColimitFreeCofan I) _ _
67	    (fun i ↦ by simp [← freeCofan_inj])
68	  right_inv f := by ext1 i; simp [← freeCofan_inj]
69	
70	lemma freeHomEquiv_comp_apply {M N : SheafOfModules.{u} R} {I : Type u}
71	    (f : free I ⟶ M) (p : M ⟶ N) (i : I) :
72	    N.freeHomEquiv (f ≫ p) i = sectionsMap p (M.freeHomEquiv f i) := rfl
73	
74	lemma freeHomEquiv_symm_comp {M N : SheafOfModules.{u} R} {I : Type u} (s : I → M.sections)
75	    (p : M ⟶ N) :
76	    M.freeHomEquiv.symm s ≫ p = N.freeHomEquiv.symm (fun i ↦ sectionsMap p (s i)) :=
77	  N.freeHomEquiv.injective (by ext; simp [freeHomEquiv_comp_apply])
78	
79	/-- The tautological section of `free I : SheafOfModules R` corresponding to `i : I`. -/
80	noncomputable abbrev freeSection {I : Type u} (i : I) : (free (R := R) I).sections :=
81	  (free (R := R) I).freeHomEquiv (𝟙 (free I)) i
82	
83	lemma freeHomEquiv_apply {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} {I : Type u}
84	    (f : free I ⟶ M) (i : I) :
85	    freeHomEquiv M f i = sectionsMap f (freeSection i) :=
86	  rfl
87	
88	lemma unitHomEquiv_symm_freeHomEquiv_apply
89	    {I : Type u} {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (f : free I ⟶ M) (i : I) :
90	    M.unitHomEquiv.symm (M.freeHomEquiv f i) = ιFree i ≫ f := by
91	  simp [freeHomEquiv]
92	
93	section
94	
95	variable {I J : Type u} (f : I → J)
96	
97	/-- The morphism of presheaves of `R`-modules `free I ⟶ free J` induced by
98	a map `f : I → J`. -/
99	noncomputable def freeMap : free (R := R) I ⟶ free J :=
100	  (freeHomEquiv _).symm (fun i ↦ freeSection (f i))
101	
102	@[simp]
103	lemma freeHomEquiv_freeMap :
104	    (freeHomEquiv _ (freeMap (R := R) f)) = freeSection.comp f :=
105	  (freeHomEquiv _).symm.injective (by simp; rfl)
106	
107	@[simp]
108	lemma sectionMap_freeMap_freeSection (i : I) :
109	    sectionsMap (freeMap (R := R) f) (freeSection i) = freeSection (f i) := by
110	  simp [← freeHomEquiv_comp_apply]
111	
112	lemma sectionsMap_freeHomEquiv_symm_freeSection
113	    {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (f : I → M.sections) (i : I) :
114	    sectionsMap ((freeHomEquiv M).symm f) (freeSection i) = f i := by
115	  obtain ⟨f, rfl⟩ := (freeHomEquiv M).surjective f
116	  cat_disch
117	
118	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
119	lemma ιFree_freeMap (i : I) :
120	    ιFree (R := R) i ≫ freeMap f = ιFree (f i) := by
121	  rw [← unitHomEquiv_symm_freeHomEquiv_apply, freeHomEquiv_freeMap]
122	  dsimp [freeSection]
123	  rw [unitHomEquiv_symm_freeHomEquiv_apply, Category.comp_id]
124	
125	end

129	presheaf of modules over itself). -/

/-- The value of the scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd a` on a section `x` over `Y` is
multiplication by the restriction of `a`: `(scalarEnd a)(x) = x · a|_Y`. Project-local
helper, the computational heart of the `scalarEnd` ring-hom identities below. -/
lemma scalarEnd_val_app (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) (Y : (TopologicalSpace.Opens (X : TopCat))ᵒᵖ)
    (x : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y) :
    (scalarEnd a).val.app Y x = x * X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a := by
  have h1 : (scalarEnd a).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
      = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a := by
    have := (SheafOfModules.unitHomEquiv_apply_coe (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
      (scalarEnd a) Y).symm
    rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.apply_symm_apply] at this
    exact this.symm
  calc (scalarEnd a).val.app Y x
      = (scalarEnd a).val.app Y (x • (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)) := by
        rw [smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
    _ = x • (scalarEnd a).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y) := by
        rw [map_smul]
    _ = x • X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a := by rw [h1]
    _ = x * X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a := smul_eq_mul _ _

/-- Composition of scalar endomorphisms is multiplication: `scalarEnd a ≫ scalarEnd b =
scalarEnd (a * b)`. Project-local; underlies `matrixEnd` multiplicativity. -/
lemma scalarEnd_comp (a b : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
    scalarEnd a ≫ scalarEnd b = scalarEnd (a * b) := by
  apply (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv.injective
  ext Y
  change (scalarEnd b).val.app Y ((scalarEnd a).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y))
      = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (a * b)
  rw [scalarEnd_val_app, scalarEnd_val_app, one_mul, map_mul]
  ring

/-- `scalarEnd` is additive: `scalarEnd (a + b) = scalarEnd a + scalarEnd b`.
Project-local; underlies `matrixEnd` matrix-multiplication identity. -/
lemma scalarEnd_add (a b : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
    scalarEnd (a + b) = scalarEnd a + scalarEnd b := by
  apply (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv.injective
  ext Y
  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (a + b)
      = (scalarEnd a + scalarEnd b).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
  rw [SheafOfModules.add_val, PresheafOfModules.add_app]
  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (a + b)
      = (scalarEnd a).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
        + (scalarEnd b).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
  rw [scalarEnd_val_app, scalarEnd_val_app, one_mul, one_mul, map_add]

/-- `scalarEnd` of a finite sum is the sum of the `scalarEnd`s. Project-local. -/
lemma scalarEnd_sum {ι : Type*} (s : Finset ι) (f : ι → Γ(X, ⊤)) :
    scalarEnd (∑ i ∈ s, f i) = ∑ i ∈ s, scalarEnd (f i) := by
  classical
  induction s using Finset.induction with
  | empty => simp [scalarEnd_zero]
  | insert a s ha ih => rw [Finset.sum_insert ha, Finset.sum_insert ha, scalarEnd_add, ih]

lemma scalarEnd_val_app (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) (Y : (TopologicalSpace.Opens (X : TopCat))ᵒᵖ)
    (x : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y) :
    (scalarEnd a).val.app Y x = x * X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a := by
  have h1 : (scalarEnd a).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
      = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a := by
    have := (SheafOfModules.unitHomEquiv_apply_coe (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
      (scalarEnd a) Y).symm
    rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.apply_symm_apply] at this
    exact this.symm
  calc (scalarEnd a).val.app Y x
      = (scalarEnd a).val.app Y (x • (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)) := by
        rw [smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
    _ = x • (scalarEnd a).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y) := by
        rw [map_smul]
    _ = x • X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a := by rw [h1]
    _ = x * X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a := smul_eq_mul _ _
input.new_string:
lemma scalarEnd_val_app (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) (Y : (TopologicalSpace.Opens (X : TopCat))ᵒᵖ)
    (x : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y) :
    (scalarEnd a).val.app Y x = x * X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a := by
  have h1 : (scalarEnd a).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
      = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a := by
    have e := SheafOfModules.unitHomEquiv_apply_coe (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
      (scalarEnd a) Y
    rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.apply_symm_apply] at e
    rw [scalarEnd, ← e]
  have hsmul : (scalarEnd a).val.app Y (x • (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y))
      = x • (scalarEnd a).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y) := by
    rw [ConcreteCategory.hom]; exact map_smul _ _ _
  calc (scalarEnd a).val.app Y x
      = (scalarEnd a).val.app Y (x • (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)) := by
        rw [smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
    _ = x • (scalarEnd a).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y) := hsmul
    _ = x • X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a := by rw [h1]
    _ = x * X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a := smul_eq_mul _ _

/-! ## Matrix automorphisms of the free sheaf

To realise the `GL_d` bundle transitions we promote an (invertible) `d × d` matrix of
global sections to an automorphism of the free rank-`d` sheaf `O_S^d`, exactly as
`chartQuotientMap` realises the universal matrix. The two key algebraic facts — that
`matrixEnd` turns matrix multiplication into composition and the identity matrix into the
identity — follow from the `scalarEnd` ring-hom identities above. -/

/-- `SheafOfModules` over `O_S` has finite biproducts (it has finite products). -/
instance hasFiniteBiproducts_modules (S : Scheme.{0}) :
    HasFiniteBiproducts (SheafOfModules S.ringCatSheaf) :=
  HasFiniteBiproducts.of_hasFiniteProducts

/-- A `d × d` matrix of global sections of `O_S` realised as an endomorphism of the free
rank-`d` sheaf `O_S^d`: the `(p,q)`-entry acts as `scalarEnd`, assembled over the rank-`d`
biproduct (mirrors `chartQuotientMap`). Project-local. -/
noncomputable def matrixEnd {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
    SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) ⟶
      SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) :=
  (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).symm.hom ≫
    biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (M p i)) ≫
    (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).hom

/-- Composition of two `biproduct.matrix` morphisms is the matrix of pointwise sums of
composites — the categorical matrix product. Project-local helper for `matrixEnd_comp`. -/
private lemma biproduct_matrix_comp {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
    (mM mN : Fin d → Fin d →
      (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)) :
    biproduct.matrix (f := fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)
        (g := fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) mM ≫ biproduct.matrix mN
      = biproduct.matrix (fun i q => ∑ p, mM i p ≫ mN p q) := by
  refine biproduct.hom_ext' _ _ (fun i => biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun q => ?_))
  rw [biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.matrix_components, Category.assoc,
    biproduct.matrix_π, biproduct.lift_desc]

/-- `matrixEnd` turns matrix multiplication into composition (with the order reversed by
the contravariance of the column/component indexing): `matrixEnd M ≫ matrixEnd N =
matrixEnd (N * M)`. Project-local. -/
lemma matrixEnd_comp {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
    matrixEnd M ≫ matrixEnd N = matrixEnd (N * M) := by
  rw [matrixEnd, matrixEnd, matrixEnd, Category.assoc, Category.assoc, Category.assoc]
  rw [show (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).hom ≫
        (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).symm.hom ≫
        biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (N p i)) ≫
        (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).hom
      = biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (N p i)) ≫
        (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).hom by
      rw [Iso.symm_hom, ← Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.id_comp]]
  rw [← Category.assoc (biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (M p i))), biproduct_matrix_comp]
  congr 2
  funext i q
  rw [show (fun p => scalarEnd (M p i) ≫ scalarEnd (N p q)) = _ from rfl]
  simp_rw [scalarEnd_comp]
  rw [← scalarEnd_sum]
  congr 1
  rw [Matrix.mul_apply]
  exact Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun p _ => mul_comm _ _)

/-- `matrixEnd` of the identity matrix is the identity. Project-local. -/
lemma matrixEnd_one {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} :
    matrixEnd (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) = 𝟙 _ := by
  rw [matrixEnd]
  have hmat : biproduct.matrix
        (fun i p => scalarEnd ((1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) p i))
      = 𝟙 (⨁ fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) := by
    refine biproduct.hom_ext' _ _ (fun i => biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun p => ?_))
    rw [biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.matrix_components, Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp,
      Matrix.one_apply]
    by_cases h : p = i
    · subst h; rw [if_pos rfl, scalarEnd_one, biproduct.ι_π_self]
    · rw [if_neg h, scalarEnd_zero, biproduct.ι_π_ne _ (Ne.symm h)]
  rw [hmat, Category.id_comp, Iso.symm_hom, Iso.inv_hom_id]

/-- An invertible `d × d` matrix of global sections induces an automorphism of the free
rank-`d` sheaf `O_S^d`. Project-local — the `GL_d` data underlying the bundle transitions. -/
noncomputable def matrixToFreeIso {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
    (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) (hMN : M * N = 1) (hNM : N * M = 1) :
    SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) ≅
      SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) where
  hom := matrixEnd M
  inv := matrixEnd N
  hom_inv_id := by rw [matrixEnd_comp, hNM, matrixEnd_one]
  inv_hom_id := by rw [matrixEnd_comp, hMN, matrixEnd_one]

@[simp] lemma matrixToFreeIso_hom {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
    (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) (hMN : M * N = 1) (hNM : N * M = 1) :
    (matrixToFreeIso M N hMN hNM).hom = matrixEnd M := rfl

488	/-- **Pullback preserves rank-`d` local freeness.** If `M` is locally free of rank `d` on
489	`T`, then `φ^* M` is locally free of rank `d` on `T'`, for any scheme morphism `φ`. The
490	chart cover `{U i}` of `T` trivialising `M` pulls back to the cover `{φ⁻¹ U i}` of `T'`;
491	on each member the restriction of `φ^* M` is identified with the pulled-back chart-free
492	sheaf via the pseudofunctor comparison `pullbackComp`, the factorisation
493	`φ ∘ (φ⁻¹ U i).ι = (φ ∣_ U i) ≫ (U i).ι` (`morphismRestrict_ι`), and `pullbackFreeIso`.

/-- The free-pullback comparison is natural in the base morphism: equal morphisms give

lemma pullbackFreeIso_eqToHom {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ) (I : Type u) :
    eqToHom (congrArg
        (fun α => (Scheme.Modules.pullback α).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)) h)
        ≫ (pullbackFreeIso ψ I).hom
      = (pullbackFreeIso φ I).hom := by
  subst h; simp

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

/-! ## The universal quotient sheaf and the tautological quotient -/

/-- The **universal quotient sheaf** `U` on `Gr(d,r)` (`def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf`):
input.new_string:
namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

/-! ## The GL_d bundle transition cocycle

The universal quotient `U` is glued from the per-chart free rank-`d` sheaves `O_{U^I}^d`
along the bundle transitions `g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)⁻¹`, realised as matrix automorphisms via
`matrixToFreeIso` and conjugated to the overlap pullbacks by `pullbackFreeIso`. This section
constructs `bundleTransition` and proves its self-identity (C1); the triple-overlap
multiplicativity (C2) is the matrix cocycle of `lem:gr_cocycle` transported to the common
overlap by `pullbackBaseChangeTransport`/`glueData_bridge_*`. -/

/-- The Cramer inverse of the self-minor `X^I_I` is the identity: since `X^I_I = 1`
(`universalMatrix_submatrix_self`) its inverse is `1`. Project-local; underlies C1. -/
lemma universalMinorInv_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
    universalMinorInv d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
  have hmin : universalMinor d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
    rw [universalMinor, universalMatrix_submatrix_self, Matrix.map_one _ (map_zero _) (map_one _)]
  rw [universalMinorInv, hmin, inv_one]

/-- The **bundle transition** `g_{I,J}` (`def:gr_bundleTransition`): the isomorphism of
sheaves of modules on the overlap `U^I_J` induced by the invertible matrix
`(X^I_J)⁻¹ = universalMinorInv d r I J`. It identifies the pullback of `O_{U^I}^d` along
`f_{IJ}` with the pullback of `O_{U^J}^d` along `t_{IJ} ≫ f_{JI}`, by conjugating the
matrix automorphism `matrixToFreeIso (X^I_J)⁻¹` (built like `chartQuotientMap`) by the
free-pullback comparisons `pullbackFreeIso`. -/
noncomputable def bundleTransition (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r))
    (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ)).obj
        (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) ≅
      (Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartTransition d r I J hI hJ ≫ chartIncl d r J I hJ hI)).obj
        (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r J).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) :=
  Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (Fin d) ≪≫
    matrixToFreeIso
      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
          (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
        (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ))
      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
          (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
        (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ))
      (by rw [← map_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1, map_one])
      (by rw [← map_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).2, map_one]) ≪≫
    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso
      (chartTransition d r I J hI hJ ≫ chartIncl d r J I hJ hI) (Fin d)).symm

/-- **Self-identity of the bundle transition (C1)** (`lem:gr_bundleCocycle_id`): on the
diagonal overlap `U^I_I` (where `t_{II} = 𝟙`) the bundle transition is the identity, in the
form required by the gluing primitive `Scheme.Modules.glue`. The matrix part is the identity
since `(X^I_I)⁻¹ = 1` (`universalMinorInv_self`), so `matrixEnd 1 = 𝟙` (`matrixEnd_one`); the
two free-pullback comparisons then cancel into the `eqToHom` transport
(`pullbackFreeIso_eqToHom`). -/
theorem bundleTransition_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
    bundleTransition d r I I hI hI
      = eqToIso (congrArg
          (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj
            (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)))
          (show chartIncl d r I I hI hI
              = chartTransition d r I I hI hI ≫ chartIncl d r I I hI hI from by
            rw [chartTransition_self, Category.id_comp])) := by
  have hφ : chartIncl d r I I hI hI
      = chartTransition d r I I hI hI ≫ chartIncl d r I I hI hI := by
    rw [chartTransition_self, Category.id_comp]
  have hone : matrixEnd ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
          (universalMinorInv d r I I hI hI))
      = 𝟙 (SheafOfModules.free (R := (chartOverlap d r I I hI hI).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) := by
    rw [universalMinorInv_self, map_one, matrixEnd_one]
  apply Iso.ext
  simp only [bundleTransition, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.symm_hom, matrixToFreeIso_hom, eqToIso.hom]
  rw [hone, Category.id_comp, ← pullbackFreeIso_eqToHom hφ, Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id,
    Category.comp_id]

/-- The bundle transition data packaged over the Grassmannian glue datum, ready to feed the
gluing primitive `Scheme.Modules.glue`. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def bundleTransitionData (d r : ℕ) :
    ∀ (I J : (theGlueData d r).J),
      (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).f I J)).obj
          (SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) ≅
        (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).t I J ≫ (theGlueData d r).f J I)).obj
          (SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U J).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) :=
  fun I J => bundleTransition d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2

/-- **Triple-overlap multiplicativity of the bundle transition (C2)**
(`lem:gr_bundleCocycle_mul`): over each triple overlap the base-change transports of the
three bundle transitions satisfy `ĝ_{JK} ∘ ĝ_{IJ} = ĝ_{IK}`, in the form required by
`Scheme.Modules.glue`. At the matrix level this is the Cramer-inverse cocycle
`(X^J_K)⁻¹ (X^I_J)⁻¹ = (X^I_K)⁻¹` (`lem:gr_cocycle`, `universalMinorInv`); the substantive
work is transporting the three transitions to the common triple overlap via
`pullbackBaseChangeTransport` and aligning endpoints with `glueData_bridge_*`.

NOTE (scaffold): the body is the remaining hard step of the bundle cocycle (~50-100 LOC of
matrix-to-module transport bookkeeping); the signature is the exact `_hC2` hypothesis of
`Scheme.Modules.glue` instantiated at `theGlueData d r` and `bundleTransitionData`. -/
theorem bundleTransition_cocycle (d r : ℕ) (I J K : (theGlueData d r).J) :
    Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport
        (Limits.pullback.fst ((theGlueData d r).f I J) ((theGlueData d r).f I K))
        ((theGlueData d r).f I J) ((theGlueData d r).t I J ≫ (theGlueData d r).f J I)
        (bundleTransitionData d r I J) ≪≫
      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr
          (Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_mid (theGlueData d r) I J K)).app _ ≪≫
      Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport
        ((theGlueData d r).t' I J K ≫
          Limits.pullback.fst ((theGlueData d r).f J K) ((theGlueData d r).f J I))
        ((theGlueData d r).f J K) ((theGlueData d r).t J K ≫ (theGlueData d r).f K J)
        (bundleTransitionData d r J K) ≪≫
      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr
          (Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_tgt (theGlueData d r) I J K)).app _
    = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr
          (Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_src (theGlueData d r) I J K)).app _ ≪≫
      Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport
        (Limits.pullback.snd ((theGlueData d r).f I J) ((theGlueData d r).f I K))
        ((theGlueData d r).f I K) ((theGlueData d r).t I K ≫ (theGlueData d r).f K I)
        (bundleTransitionData d r I K) := by
  sorry

## Blueprint dependency graph (leandag)

You can navigate the project's dependency graph read-only to find the
proven lemmas/defs your goal can lean on (with the exact `\lean{}`
names to apply) instead of re-deriving them. `archon` is on PATH;

It is a navigation aid only — the proof obligation and your blueprint

Active prover mode: **prove**

## Your goal

Fill `sorry` placeholders with complete Lean proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.

## Workflow

1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it).
2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, and lemmas already found.
3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading it is often the fastest path forward.
5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
6. **Always save partial progress.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile, but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that erases real work.
7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.

**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.

## Protected declarations

Read `archon-protected.yaml` before touching any declaration. You may fill proof bodies of protected declarations but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.

## Avoid early termination

- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
- **Decompose**: break into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available) and solve each individually.
- **Hard bar is a minimum, not a ceiling.** If your objectives specify a "hard bar" (e.g. "add def + pin signature"), that tells you the minimum required — not where to stop. After meeting it:
  - If a recipe exists in `analogies/`, the blueprint chapter has a concrete proof sketch, or you can reason about an approach: attempt the proof body immediately.
  - If no recipe exists: formulate a strategy, attempt it, and leave a partial tactic block at the stuck point. A failed attempt with a `by ... sorry` is far more useful than a clean `sorry`.
  - A bare `sorry` with no attempt is only acceptable when the mathematical obstacle is explicitly named and at least one concrete approach has been tried and documented.
- **After closing all assigned sorries, keep going.** Scan your file for other open `sorry`s that share the mathematical context you've just developed. If you can see a direct path to any of them using lemmas or infrastructure you've already built, attempt them. Stopping at the assigned task boundary when adjacent work is tractable is artificial throttling — you have write permission over your whole file.
- **Comments are not progress. Code is.** If you can describe an approach precisely enough to write it as a comment or TODO, you can attempt it. Any `-- TODO: try X`, `-- could use Y here`, or `/- Next step: ... -/` you write is a sign you stopped too early — attempt X/Y first; only leave the comment if the attempt fails with a named error. The same applies to approaches proposed in task results: if you wrote it in your log as "a possible route", you should have tried it.
- **Autonomous decision-making within your file.** You are fully authorized to make every tactical and mathematical decision within your assigned `.lean` file without waiting for the planner. This includes: choosing proof strategies, adding helper lemmas, attempting adjacent sorries, selecting alternative approaches, deciding proof structure. **Never write "I'll let the planner decide", "I'll defer to the next iteration", or "this requires planner input" for any choice that is entirely within your file.** The planner is not on call; deferring wastes the entire context-loading investment of this session.

  The ONLY actions that require stopping rather than acting:
  - Modifying a *signature* of a protected declaration (you may still fill its proof body).
  - Editing another agent's `.lean` file.
  - Removing an existing public declaration that other files import.
  - Changing the type of an existing declaration in a way that would break other files.

  Everything else — including trying a strategy the planner didn't explicitly endorse — is your call to make and attempt now.

## When to stop

Stop only when progress is blocked by a specific, named obstacle in one of these categories:

1. A genuinely missing Mathlib ingredient — named precisely, attempted via informal agent, no workaround found.
2. A definition or signature that requires another prover's output — name the specific declaration and file.
3. A proof that requires fundamental restructuring of a protected or cross-file definition — name it.

"It seems hard", "the proof is complex", "I couldn't find the right lemma", or "the planner should weigh in on this approach" are NOT valid stop reasons. Document the specific obstacle and keep trying. Only stop when you can write: "I tried approaches X, Y, Z; each fails because [specific mathematical reason]; the informal agent suggested [route] which requires [specific missing ingredient]."

**Specifically banned stop phrases:**
- "I'll wait for the planner to decide."
- "This strategy should be confirmed by the next iteration."
- "I'll defer this choice to the planner."
- "The planner may want to reconsider the approach."

If you find yourself writing any of these: delete the sentence, pick the most promising route, and attempt it. The planner will correct the choice next iter if it was wrong — a failed attempt costs nothing compared to an idle session.

## Completion criteria

Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:

1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
2. Zero axioms introduced.
3. The file compiles cleanly.

## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof

When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature**. Three patterns are banned:

- **Reflexive-iso placeholder** — replacing `X ≅ Y` with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`.
- **`Classical.choice` around an explicit witness** — dissolving `Type` vs `Prop` friction without actually constructing the witness.
- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`** — discards the declaration post-elaboration, breaking blueprint cross-references.

**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, ship the typed `sorry` instead.

## When infrastructure is missing

Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before filing "Infrastructure missing":

1. **Use the informal agent** if an API key is available (check `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` first): call `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` with "Prove [goal] using only current Mathlib." Formalize whatever it suggests. If no key is set, skip to step 2.
2. **If the missing ingredient is ≤~100 LOC**: write it as a project-local helper — typed signature plus a genuine proof attempt. A partial proof body is better than a documented gap.
3. **Only after steps 1–2 are exhausted**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` with what you tried, why it failed, and the precise statement of the missing ingredient for the plan agent to assign in `mathlib-build` mode.

"Infrastructure missing" as a verdict requires: informal agent called (or key unavailable, documented), local helper attempted if in scope. "I don't see how" is not evidence — a failed attempt with `sorry` at the stuck step IS evidence.

When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.

**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.

## Proof style

- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is display-level only. Default to `change` when in doubt.

## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md

The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:

- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.

## LSP MCP tools

The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (`mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal`, `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`, etc.). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, …) — same tools, never shell binaries.

- Always invoke through your tool-call interface.
- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` — there is no such shell command.
- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If `success: false`, retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.

## Search protocol

1. `lean_local_search` first.
2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.

## Tooling traps

- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading. Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors. Rely on `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.

## Logging

Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):

```markdown
# Algebra/WLocal.lean

## wLocal_iff (line 45)
### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance
- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing

### Attempt 2
- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
- **Result:** RESOLVED
- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
```

One section per theorem/lemma. Each attempt: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results.

## End-of-session handoff

**Before declaring done, run this self-review:**

1. Did I attempt every approach I wrote down as a comment, TODO, or "possible route"? If not, go back and attempt them.
2. Are there other open `sorry`s in my file I could attempt with the tools and lemmas I just developed?
3. Did I call the informal agent (or document that no key was available) before filing "Infrastructure missing"?
4. Is there any approach I thought of but skipped because "it would take too long" or "the planner should decide"? If it's in my file's scope, attempt it.

Only proceed to write the task result when all four answers are "yes" or "not applicable."

Before writing results:

1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles.
3. **Write a `## Summary` section** stating: sorry count before → after; exact names of sorries closed; exact names of sorries still open and why; whether you attempted adjacent sorries beyond the assigned ones.
4. **Write a `## Why I stopped` section** — be brutally honest; the planner reads this. **Only Lean code changes count as progress** — a sorry closed, a helper lemma that compiles, a partial tactic block at a specific stuck point. Comments, docstrings, TODO notes, and task result prose do NOT count. If you wrote approaches in comments that you didn't attempt, say so explicitly:
   - `Real progress`: closed N sorries — name each one. State the sorry count before and after.
   - `Partial progress`: made measurable code progress (decomposed into sub-lemmas with proof bodies, closed one branch of a case split) but did not fully close. Name the specific advance and the specific remaining blocker. Decomposing a `sorry` into N named `sorry`s with only comments in their bodies is NOT partial progress unless the bodies contain genuine proof attempts.
   - `Cosmetics only`: changed formatting/comments/style with no proof progress — say so explicitly. **Renaming a `sorry` to a named helper lemma, adding a docstring, or extracting it to a separate declaration with a `sorry` body is `Cosmetics only` unless the body contains a genuine proof attempt.**
   - `Approaches written but not attempted`: you identified routes (in comments, TODOs, or log prose) but did not attempt them — name each one and why you stopped before trying. This is a valid but weak verdict; the planner will re-assign with a directive to attempt them.
   - `Avoided the goal`: attempted something adjacent but not the assigned target — explain why and what you tried instead.
   - `Infrastructure missing`: a specific Mathlib gap was the blocker — name it precisely, confirm the informal agent was called (or key unavailable), describe what it suggested, why it failed, and state the exact missing ingredient statement.
   - `Directive not followed`: explain which part of the planner's directive you deviated from and why.

The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.

93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
128	
129	### Prover Agent
130	
131	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
132	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
133	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
134	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
135	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
136	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
137	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
138	
139	### Subagents
140	
141	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
142	
143	### Review Agent
144	
145	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
146	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
147	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
148	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
149	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
150	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
151	
152	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
153	
154	These run automatically between phases:
155	
156	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
157	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
158	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
